# Directory: tests/fixtures/ab-corpus/

## Purpose
Self-contained, sealed corpus + tooling + raw results of pre-registered A/B experiment 075
(sarcasm & multi-critic effectiveness; protocol = audit-067 Appendix A, verdicts in
`docs/reviews/ab-experiment-075.md`, human-readable methodology in local `README.md` (EN) /
`README.ru.md` (RU) — cross-linked language pair).
**Sealed:** any edit to `files/*` or `ground_truth.json` invalidates `seal.json` — re-run
`build_ground_truth.py` (new seal) and discard ALL prior runs in `results/`.

## Python modules

### files/ — review corpus (10 standalone modules, no imports between them)
Seeded files carry exactly 3 bugs each (1 logic + 1 security + 1 performance; lines/severities in `ground_truth.json`):

- `f1_auth_service.py` — auth/sessions. Seeded: inverted session-expiry check (L/CRIT), SQL injection via f-string (S/CRIT), full-table scan in `find_user_by_email` (P/HIGH).
- `f2_payments.py` — order charging/reports. Seeded: float money math with per-line rounding drift (L/HIGH), PAN+CVV logged (S/CRIT), N+1 query in `monthly_revenue_report` (P/HIGH).
- `f3_upload_handler.py` — chunked uploads. Seeded: off-by-one drops last chunk in `assemble` (L/MED), path traversal via client filename (S/CRIT), whole-file read for checksum (P/MED).
- `f4_cache_layer.py` — dict-over-Redis cache. Seeded: inverted `_is_expired` (L/HIGH), `pickle.loads` on Redis bytes (S/CRIT), unbounded L1 dict — no eviction (P/HIGH; the only bug missed by every single-reviewer arm).
- `f5_report_generator.py` — HTML reports. Seeded: boundary day excluded vs docstring contract (L/MED), XSS via unescaped display_name (S/HIGH), O(n²) string concat (P/MED).
- `f6_user_search.py` — directory search. Seeded: 1-based pagination skips first page (L/MED), ReDoS via user-supplied regex (S/HIGH), Python-side sort/slice instead of SQL LIMIT (P/MED).
- `f7_task_worker.py` — queue worker. Seeded: retry counter reset inside loop → infinite retry (L/CRIT), `subprocess shell=True` with payload data (S/CRIT), blocking `time.sleep` in async `poll_loop` (P/HIGH).
- `f8_config_loader.py` — YAML+env config. Seeded: file/env precedence inverted vs docstring (L/MED), `yaml.load` without SafeLoader (S/CRIT), full re-read+re-parse on every `get()` (P/MED).
- `c1_text_utils.py` — clean control #1 (text helpers; intentionally bug-free).
- `c2_date_utils.py` — clean control #2 (tz-aware date helpers; intentionally bug-free).

### Tooling
- `build_ground_truth.py` — one-shot generator: locates each seeded bug by unique anchor substring (+occurrence index), derives line numbers (never hand-counted), asserts stratification (8/8/8 by class AND by severity), writes `ground_truth.json` + sha256 `seal.json`. Re-running re-seals.
- `analyze.py` — frozen-pre-data scorer. `match_bug()` = same file + |Δline|≤3 + class equal; `load_d()` = deterministic Phase-2 merge of arm-D critic trios (dedup rule 1 + bikeshedding rule 4); `score_arm()` = per-run recall/FP/bikeshedding + pooled breakdowns; `main()` evaluates the 3 pre-registered decision rules, prints the summary table, writes `analysis.json`. Run: `python3 analyze.py` (no args, reads siblings).

## Data artifacts
- `ground_truth.json` — sealed answer key: 24 bugs × {id, file, line, class, severity, description} + controls list + stratification counts.
- `seal.json` — sha256 of all 10 corpus files + ground_truth.json, with UTC seal timestamp (2026-06-10T10:50:01Z — predates first run).
- `scan_floor.json` — raw `run_audit.py` output on `files/` (deterministic regex floor: catches 4/8 security bugs + 4 noise findings).
- `scan_summary.txt` — compact per-finding lines; per-file slices were injected into arm-D security-critic prompts (v3.20.5 evidence contract).
- `analysis.json` — full scorer output: per-arm per-run metrics, pooled class/severity breakdowns, decision-rule verdicts.
- `results/A|B|C|E/<file>__r<k>.json` — 120 single-reviewer reports `{arm, file, run, findings[], convergence}`.
- `results/D/<file>__r<k>/{logic,security,performance}.json` — 90 raw critic reports (schema-validated; merged only at scoring time).
- `results/wallclock.log` — start/done stamps, token totals and durations per arm.
- `results/token_split.json` — per-arm input/output/cache-write/cache-read/API-call totals, aggregated post-hoc from agent-transcript usage blocks (primary source for token accounting; harness aggregate counters differ by ~2.8%).

## Invariants
1. Seal-before-run: `seal.json` timestamp must predate every result file.
2. Reviewer agents never see `ground_truth.json`, `seal.json`, `.AGENTS.md`, or `README.md` — only one file from `files/`.
3. `analyze.py` semantics are part of the pre-registration — do not "improve" matching rules post-hoc; a new scoring idea = a new experiment.
4. Cross-seal comparisons are invalid (different corpus hash ⇒ different experiment).
